name: healthcare_satellite_derived_southport_auto_ben
premise: A hospital incident command bridge convened after a critical infrastructure or patient-flow failure with competing clinical priorities. Bed capacity, consent, and chart documentation are all under real-time scrutiny. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: healthcare satellite derived southport auto ben. The moral tension is explicitly framed as autonomy versus beneficence: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Pager queues are saturated; middle management is forwarding screenshots from an internal channel that is already leaking. The breach-notification and customer-communication clock has started; executive staff expect a single coherent position before the next press cycle. Anyone affected by an automated hold or model output must retain a human escalation path that is actually staffed. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible.

agents.name: Mira Chandra
agents.identity: I am Mira Chandra, the accountable decision-maker on this Healthcare bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes beneficence whenever someone invokes autonomy.

agents.name: Ravi Navarro
agents.identity: I am Ravi Navarro, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so beneficence is not sacrificed by default just because autonomy feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect beneficence.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Mira Chandra must decide under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Mira Chandra do?
